description: In 2013, Seattle voters and city council members engaged in a contentious debate over raising the minimum wage to $15 per hour. The initiative, led by Kshama Sawant and labor activists, culminated in a June 2014 vote by the City Council to adopt the increase, marking the first major U.S. city to implement such a high local wage floor.

key_events.description: Kshama Sawant wins a seat on the Seattle City Council on a $15 platform, signaling mainstream political viability.
Seattle Chamber of Commerce releases a report warning of massive job losses, causing a temporary dip in support.
Mayor Ed Murray introduces his compromise implementation plan, softening the blow for small businesses.
Seattle City Council votes 9-0 to approve the ordinance, locking in the final pro-leaning consensus.

calibration_notes: This scenario captures the transition of a fringe policy demand into municipal law through a high-stakes legislative campaign, illustrating how initial business opposition eventually yields to political compromise.
